title: EPPP-guided independent QA review: non-EPPP group B
verdict: pass-with-major-editorial-priorities-and-declared-independent-content-gaps - the corrected final snapshot has zero open hard factual, answer-key, source, scope, linkage, blueprint, provenance, accessibility-boundary, or deployment-parity defects; six packs still need 2,467 new independent question kernels in total, and feedback specificity, clue resistance, and learning-library depth remain material editorial work.

epppComparator.interpretation: EPPP supplied the editorial and learning-feature comparison model, not an official validation standard. Its staged review evidence and visual depth exceed these seven libraries, while its learning-library QA itself remains review-in-progress.

reviewStandard.method: Automated hard-gate inspection covered every one of the 3,500 pack activities, all 1,700 currently independent items, every learning-library activity type, every learner-visible source reference, all native and library QA summaries, and exact source/deploy parity. Manual review adjudicated 58 highest-risk independent items, the corrected algebra knowledge check, simulation boundaries, representative workshops and learning activities, and the most repetitive library content.
reviewStandard.releaseRule: The verdict may begin with pass only when the final snapshot contains no open hard factual, key, one-best-answer, source, scope, linkage, blueprint, provenance, encoding, boundary, or parity defect. Declared independent-question gaps and screening heuristics remain transparent product/editorial priorities rather than hidden hard failures.

snapshot.meaning: Hash of the seven reviewed source-pack hashes in pack-stem order.

regressionTests.coverage: Pack registration, five-bank shape, diagnostic calculations, simulation boundaries, learning-library inventories, learning links, boundary language, corrected equation-specific rationales, generated QA, and deployment mirrors.

independentContentAccounting.interpretation: All packs honestly declare their independent/guided composition. ParaPro now meets the 500-distinct target. The other six contain five 100-activity banks but do not yet contain 500 independent question kernels each.

warningDiagnostics.interpretation: These are screening heuristics, not automatic wrong-key findings. Manual priority review found defensible keys, but the near-universal length signal in several packs materially reduces exam-likeness and should be edited.

optionFeedbackAudit.interpretation: Every required feedback field is present and no feedback was found to mark a wrong option as correct. In the six non-ParaPro packs, all 3,600 wrong-option explanations begin with the same generic wrapper, and 3,567 reproduce the complete overall rationale; this falls below the EPPP option-specific-feedback model. ParaPro feedback is choice-specific but often concise, producing 394 item-level detail warnings.

workshops.schemaResult: pass - prompts, stimuli, task parts, planning frames, criteria, pitfalls, sample outlines, sources, and non-scoring boundaries are present

schoolLibrarianRepetition.interpretation: Structurally valid but materially repetitive and well below EPPP-like instructional depth.

learningLibraryAudit.epppDepthGap: All seven libraries have zero diagrams, compared with 25 EPPP diagrams and 58 placements. A visual should be added only when it materially teaches the content and includes accessible text alternatives.

referenceCatalogAudit.interpretation: The catalog no longer exposes a bare DOI or URL, and every entry passes minimum metadata gates. Eight entries still use an obviously generic Resource/diagnostics-style name or fallback summary and should be made source-specific.

officialBlueprintVerification.packSimulation: 70 selected-response items / approximately 70-minute selected-response pacing segment
officialBlueprintVerification.alignment: pass - the separate 50-minute written-response component and four constructed responses are explicitly disclosed; eight workshops provide unscored practice
pass - the two separately timed 30-minute essays and 275-minute total are explicitly disclosed; eight workshops provide unscored practice

officialBlueprintVerification.officialFormatNote: The official assessment may include audio or video material.
officialBlueprintVerification.alignment: pass-boundary - shared official timing and separate written-response practice are disclosed; multimedia stimulus practice remains a feature gap
pass - the pack notes that the official assessment may use multiple selected-response interaction types

existingQaAssessment.reason: Existing generated QA is strong on counts, schema, source hosts, minimum length, provenance, boundaries, and linkage. It previously missed the School Librarian semantic mapper defect and the Praxis Core cross-equation rationale defect, so this review added durable correction guards and still records feedback specificity, clue resistance, and duplication as open editorial work.

sourceDeployParity.comparison: Exact byte equality between test_prep/source hub artifacts and desktop/web-app/public mirrors.

findings.title: School Librarian item-to-learning links
findings.evidence: The pre-correction snapshot exposed 480 incompatible skill/chapter links, beginning with sl5312-b1-002. The durable mapper was rebuilt; the final all-500-item scan reports zero incompatible links, all QA passes, and source/deploy mirrors are exact.

findings.title: Praxis Core algebra rationale imported an unrelated parallel equation
findings.evidence: core5752-ch-11-check-01 and the paired source items previously mixed 3x + 7 = 25 with 5x - 12 = 18 in one explanation. The durable content now gives equation-specific substitution-verified rationales, native/library QA assertions reject cross-equation text, both focused test files pass, and the corrected text is present in both hub releases.

findings.title: Wrong-option feedback is structurally complete but generic in six packs
findings.evidence: All 3,600 wrong-option explanations outside ParaPro start with Not the best answer; 3,567 contain the full overall rationale. Manual samples confirmed that the wrapper often does not diagnose the selected distractor's misconception.
findings.impact: The UI provides feedback after every answer, but much of it falls below EPPP-style option-specific remediation.

findings.title: Answer-length and specificity clues are pervasive
findings.evidence: 987 of 1,700 independent items trigger the severe answer-length heuristic; every School Psychologist item, 198 PLT items, 192 School Librarian items, and 184 Reading Specialist items trigger it. Manual priority review found defensible keys but repeatedly confirmed a longer, more nuanced key against short or extreme distractors.
findings.impact: Test-wise learners can infer answers without demonstrating the intended content knowledge.

findings.title: Only ParaPro currently meets the 500-distinct-question target
findings.evidence: The seven packs contain 3,500 activities but 1,033 distinct independent content kernels. The other 2,467 needed kernels are honestly recorded as open; guided transformations are not mislabeled as independent questions.
findings.impact: The top-level five-bank experience works, but six packs do not yet meet the user's distinct-question product goal.

findings.title: School Librarian content is unusually repetitive
findings.evidence: The 200 independent items reduce to 26 content kernels; its 48 learning sections have 26 unique normalized bodies, and its 75 flashcards have 26 unique normalized fronts.
findings.impact: Breadth counts overstate instructional variety and remediation depth.

findings.title: Learning-library key-length clues and EPPP depth gaps remain
findings.evidence: 288 of 420 knowledge checks trigger the severe key-length heuristic, all seven libraries contain zero diagrams, and the EPPP comparator contains 25 diagrams with 58 placements.
findings.impact: The libraries meet structural inventory requirements but do not yet match EPPP's visual and editorial depth.

findings.title: Eight source-catalog entries remain obviously generic
findings.evidence: Every one of 54 references has a title, organization, summary, and credibility note, but eight entries still use Resource/diagnostics-style naming or fallback summaries rather than a source-specific learner-facing description.
findings.impact: Learners can identify the publisher, but the source overlay is less informative than intended.

correctionsRecommended.action: Author 2,467 genuinely new independent question kernels across PLT K-6 (400), Praxis Core (393), Reading Specialist (400), School Counselor (400), School Librarian (474), and School Psychologist (400). Preserve blueprint balance and never relabel guided transformations as independent questions.

Rewrite the 3,600 generic wrong-option explanations in the six non-ParaPro packs as concise, selected-distractor-specific diagnoses. Review ParaPro's 394 flagged items for whether its concise feedback still names the actual misconception.

Human-edit the 987 independent-item and 288 knowledge-check severe answer-length flags. Make distractors parallel in grammar, specificity, plausibility, and length; remove qualifiers that occur only in the key; rerun the clue diagnostics.
Reauthor the School Librarian source bank and learning library to replace repeated kernels, section bodies, and flashcard fronts with domain-specific cases and misconceptions.
Replace the eight obvious catalog fallback entries with the exact source title and a brief source-specific summary; remove the misleading word diagnostics from official Study Companion titles.

Add selective accessible visuals where they materially teach a concept, and add Reading Specialist multimedia-stimulus practice or a clearer feature-gap note because the official assessment may include audio/video material.

Extend generated QA to reject generic full-rationale feedback wrappers, cross-equation or cross-prompt rationale text, low semantic variety, foreign exam codes, and pervasive clue patterns. Retain the newly added equation-specific Praxis Core guards.

Before making validity, readiness, score, or pass claims, obtain credential-specific subject-matter review, accessibility user testing, field testing, psychometric calibration, and current legal/ethical review where the domain is high stakes.

limitations: Automation inspected every one of the 3,500 pack activities and all learning-library structures, but semantic key/content adjudication was risk-based and manually covered 58 independent items rather than every one of the 1,700 independent keys.

The 1,800 guided activities were structurally checked for derivation, linkage, provenance, feedback presence, and boundaries; they were not each manually adjudicated as independent exam questions because the product correctly labels them guided practice only.
Key-length, lexical-overlap, prompt-length, and distractor-extremity counts are screening heuristics. They prioritize editing and do not by themselves prove that a key is wrong.
Official ETS materials were used to verify published counts, formats, and timing on 2026-07-18. Test specifications and jurisdictional requirements can change and should be rechecked before release.

Reference-catalog checks establish coverage and learner-facing metadata; this review did not independently validate every sentence in every external source or perform a live availability check for all 54 URLs.
No psychometric calibration, field testing, differential-item-functioning analysis, independent credential-owner approval, or licensed-professional endorsement was performed.

Some ParaPro Bank 5 mathematics content was authored earlier in this agent lineage. Separate passing cross-review evidence was required by the manifest, but this report is still not an external subject-matter-expert review.
The EPPP learning library was used as a feature and review-process comparator; its own QA status is review-in-progress, so comparison with EPPP is not equivalent to official approval.
Source/deploy parity and the snapshot hash describe the workspace immediately after the correction rebuild. Any later content generation requires a fresh parity and QA run.
